anthropic.note: Hard-coded in shared_gates.py _ANTHROPIC_DAILY_LIMIT (added 2026-04-25). Caps Haiku NC scans + ad-hoc reasoning calls. 500/day = ~$1.50/day max. Cache + 60s error cooldown protect within budget.

gemini.note: Hard-coded in gemma4_helper.py _GEMINI_DAILY_LIMIT (added 2026-04-25). Currently only audit_check + audit_fire_debate use it; cap has plenty of headroom.

ai_consensus.gemini:
**(a) Runaway loops:** Anthropic’s 60s cooldown protects only against *errors*. A logical loop of successful but flawed "ad-hoc reasoning calls" bypasses this cooldown, rapidly exhausting daily limits.

**(b) Missing caps:** Caps are enforced via local Python variables (`_ANTHROPIC_DAILY_LIMIT`). *Forecast if wrong:* If you do not configure hard global limits at the API-provider level, frequent bot restarts (which reset in-memory counters) or horizontal scaling will completely bypass these local caps, leading to unbounded spending.

**(c) 2x Rate blow-up:** Projections show 0 calls despite 20 active timers. If Gemini dynamic usage doubles, multi-turn "debates" could exceed expected token lengths, exponentially blowing up costs per call.

**(d) Silent burn:** Gemini (`audit_fire_debate`). B
ai_consensus.anthropic:
**Audit verdict: LOW is misleading — this is "unknown," not "safe."**

(a) **Runaway loops:** 20 timers discovered but `sources: []` and `calls_per_day: 0` means the auditor failed to attribute any timer to a paid service. Any timer firing Anthropic/Gemini inside a retry/except block with no backoff will hit the daily cap fast, then silently fail.

(b) **Missing caps:** Caps exist only for Anthropic + Gemini. OpenAI, embeddings, data feeds, broker APIs — uncapped. Also no per-minute or per-hour cap; 500/day can burn in 60s.

(c) **2× rate:** Anthropic → cap absorbs it ($3/day). Gemini → still under 200/day, fine. Real risk is uncapped services.

(d) **Most likely silent burner:** Haiku NC scans — high-frequency, cached, easy to miss when cache invalidates.

Fix: instrument timer→service ma
ai_consensus.grok:
**Blow-up risks (projected 0 but 20 timers unseen):**

(a) Runaway loop in any of the 20 timers hammering ad-hoc Anthropic reasoning (cache/cooldown fail) → instantly hits 500/day hard cap ($1.50/day) every day.

(b) Missing caps: Gemini only protects 2 functions; 18 timers can call freely. No enforcement on new trading logic.

(c) Double-rate blow-up: Anthropic doubles from any hidden baseline to 1000 calls = $3/day sustained. Gemini stays safe.

(d) **Anthropic is most likely to burn silently** — broad "ad-hoc reasoning" surface + only soft cache protection, no operator visibility into timer-triggered calls.

Overall: "LOW" rating is misleading; timer gap hides real risk.
